data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 8937 "Producer<->consumer snapshot contract: shared schema + round-trip test through production path"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/fe57) and corroborated against the repo. All 7 cross-child interaction modes checked under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 finding.

File-disjointness corroboration: 8937 file_impact = `_snapshot_schema.py` (new), `tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_snapshot_contract.py` (new), `fetcher.py` (producer validates vs schema). fetch_snapshot exists at fetcher.py L480. These three are DISJOINT from every sibling's edits — fe57 (tests/fixtures/jira/, _fakes.py, capture script), 59ef (test_acliclient_contract.py, acli_graph.py), 33d0 (apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py), 2359 (batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py), 822a (e2e_field_validation_probe.sh, test_roundtrip.py). No sibling co-edits fetcher.py or the two new files; fetcher.py is only READ by fe57's fake through fetch_snapshot, not co-edited.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. No sibling touches 8937's three files; the schema module is net-new and fetcher.py is consumed (read) not co-edited.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. 8937's schema pins comment(nested)/issuelinks/parent/scalars; fe57 AC2 serves fixtures through fetch_snapshot with NO shape transformation, feeding precisely the shapes 8937's schema targets — mutually reinforcing. The outbound labels list-vs-count telemetry mismatch flagged on 2359/33d0 is outbound-telemetry-internal; 8937's snapshot-entry/read-side schema neither asserts nor depends on labels_applied representation, so it is untouched.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 8937 correctly declares depends_on fe57 (round-trip needs the fixtures/fake). It explicitly scopes OUT fixtures (→fe57), applier collapse (→33d0), telemetry (→2359), all consistent with the roster; no missing input edge.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. Four stories drive "the production path" from DISTINCT angles on DISTINCT files: 8937 = assembled snapshot-entry SCHEMA + producer→consumer key/shape round-trip (test_snapshot_contract.py); 59ef = enrichment-METHOD return-shape fidelity (test_acliclient_contract.py); 33d0 = outbound-only multi-sub-op APPLICATION correctness; 822a = BIDIRECTIONAL inbound↔outbound behavioral ROUND-TRIP (test_roundtrip.py). Complementary, not duplicative; 8937 declares the others OUT consistently.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. 8937's round-trip targets "the production path" by name; 33d0's end-state IS the single production applier, so 8937's test binds to the survivor by construction (8937 does not edit the applier files — no same-region co-edit with the 33d0↔2359 outbound-applier sequencing cluster). The only hard ordering edge (fixtures before the consuming test) is captured by depends_on fe57.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. The new _snapshot_schema.py SSOT that fetcher.py validates against is additive; 8937 AC4 asserts semantic keys/types/round-trip (no whole-blob golden equality, no mock-call-count), consistent with parent AC7 "no net increase in brittle tests."
- Residual references — BENIGN. 33d0 deletes the DEAD typed-leaf applier, but 8937 exercises the PRODUCTION path, so 33d0's deletion strands nothing 8937 references.

The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNING new tests — for which 8937 is repeatedly named the natural home) is a G3 COVERAGE matter, already tracked across the sibling set and on this ticket's prior G3 audits; it is not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so it is not re-filed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 8937): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.
